content.text:
Not from my edits. **Pre-existing bug in `bud_dispatch.sh`.**

### Mechanism

- `rbw-gPI.PayorInstall.sh` sets BOTH `BURD_NO_LOG=1` AND `BURD_INTERACTIVE=1` (the only tabtarget with this combination — confirmed by grep).
- In `bud_dispatch.sh:179`, the historical-log paths (`BURD_LOG_LAST` / `BURD_LOG_SAME` / `BURD_LOG_HIST`) are only initialized when `BURD_NO_LOG` is empty. With `BURD_NO_LOG=1`, they're never set.
- In `bud_dispatch.sh:358-360`, the interactive branch runs first and references `${BURD_LOG_HIST}` (no `:-` default), so `set -u` fires the unbound-variable error.

The branch structure treats `INTERACTIVE` and `NO_LOG` as mutually exclusive (interactive wins via if/elif/else), but `rbw-gPI` sets both — and the interactive branch then assumes the logging vars exist.

### Repair I'd attempt

One-line fix at line 358: tighten the interactive-branch condition so it requires *both* INTERACTIVE=1 *and* NO_LOG=empty. Then NO_LOG+INTERACTIVE falls through to the existing elif at line 364 (the no-log branch), which is the correct semantic.

```bash
# Current:
if test -n "${BURD_INTERACTIVE:-}"; then

# Repaired:
if test -n "${BURD_INTERACTIVE:-}" && test -z "${BURD_NO_LOG:-}"; then
```

The no-log branch already does the right thing (exec the command, capture exit status, no logging). Falling through to it preserves interactive semantics (no `2>&1 | tee` redirect breaking line buffering) — interactivity isn't lost.

### Scope question

This bug is **out of scope for ₢BAAAA**. Three options:

1. **Patch as a side-effect of this pace** — slip the one-line fix into the running notch. Pragmatic but pace-scope creep; CLAUDE.md generally discourages this.
2. **Extract into a separate pace** — slate (or chivvy) a new pace in ₣BA for the dispatch fix; defer Tier 2 of ₢BAAAA until that pace lands. Clean but slower.
3. **Defer Tier 2 entirely** — accept that interactive verification can't run right now; skip to Tier 3 (which doesn't use this code path). The mechanical+regime verification already covered most of F2/F3/F5 indirectly.

Your call.

message.content: Don't worry about scope creep, we need to fix this now.  I confess I can't remember: why is this _INTERACTIVE?

content.text:
### Why `BURD_INTERACTIVE`

It signals the dispatcher to **preserve line-buffering** of the command's output so interactive prompts display immediately.

In the default (non-interactive, non-no-log) branch at line 370, the command's stdout flows through a `while IFS= read -r z_line; do ...` loop that copies each line to three log files plus stdout. That `read` only delivers a line once a newline arrives — so prompts like `Copy the authorization code and paste here: ` (no trailing newline) get buffered until the user types and presses Enter. The user never sees the prompt. They appear to be staring at a hung process.

`BURD_INTERACTIVE=1` switches to a `tee` redirect, which writes through as bytes arrive — line buffering preserved, prompts display promptly. The trade-off is `tee` writes raw uncurated bytes, so logs include things like ANSI escapes the curated path strips.

`BURD_NO_LOG=1` is orthogonal: it says "don't write log files at all" (used here because credentials and OAuth-related output flow through). The two flags **were intended to be combinable** — "interactive prompts AND no logging" is the right semantics for credential-handling ceremonies — but the dispatcher's else-if chain treats them as mutually exclusive and the interactive branch then assumes log paths exist. That's the bug.

The fix makes them properly composable: interactive + no-log → fall through to the no-log branch, which already preserves line buffering (the command runs in a plain brace group, no `while-read`).

Applying now.

    zBURD_EXIT_STATUS=${PIPESTATUS[0]}
    echo "${zBURD_EXIT_STATUS}" > "${zBURD_STATUS_FILE}"
    zbud_show "Coordinator status (interactive): ${zBURD_EXIT_STATUS}"
  elif test -n "${BURD_NO_LOG:-}"; then

content.text: Three files dirty as expected. Notching with intent that names these as the two pre-existing bugs surfaced by Tier 2 (folded into ₢BAAAA per operator authorization).

input.params: {"identity": "BAAAA", "files": ["Tools/buk/bud_dispatch.sh", "Tools/rbk/rbz_zipper.sh", "Tools/rbk/rbk-claude-tabtarget-context.md"], "intent": "Fix two pre-existing bugs surfaced by Tier 2 verification of ₢BAAAA's F2/F5/F3 edits (operator authorized in-scope). Bug 1 (bud_dispatch.sh:358): the dispatcher's interactive branch unconditionally referenced BURD_LOG_HIST at the tee pipeline, but BURD_LOG_HIST is only initialized when BURD_NO_LOG is empty (line 179). The rbw-gPI tabtarget intentionally sets BOTH BURD_NO_LOG=1 (credentials must not be logged) and BURD_INTERACTIVE=1 (preserve line-buffered prompt display so the auth-code paste prompt actually appears), and was the only tabtarget hitting this combination. Result was 'BURD_LOG_HIST: unbound variable' from set -u before rbgp_payor_install could even start. Fix tightens the interactive-branch condition from `test -n BURD_INTERACTIVE` to `test -n BURD_INTERACTIVE && test -z BURD_NO_LOG`, so the NO_LOG+INTERACTIVE combination falls through to the existing NO_LOG elif branch — which already preserves line buffering (no while-read curating loop) and skips log writes. The two flags are now properly composable as originally intended. Bug 2 (rbz_zipper.sh:79): the rbw-gPI colophon was enrolled with channel '' (no folio), but rbgp_payor_install takes a JSON path as $1 — the workbench therefore stripped the argument and emitted 'Colophon takes no folio; ignoring unexpected argument(s)' before rbgp_payor_install died on missing arg. Fix changes the enrollment channel from '' to 'param1', matching the pattern used by neighbor enrollments like rbw-dE (param1, takes vessel moniker) and rbw-dY (param1, takes stamp). Also regenerated rbk-claude-tabtarget-context.md via rbw-MG.MarshalGenerate to reflect the channel change (single-line diff in the Guide table: rbw-gPI Folio column now shows 'param1' instead of '—'); this keeps rbq_qualify_fast's live-vs-committed diff clean. Tier 2 retry pending operator action; will run fast-suite regression check after Tier 2 succeeds to backstop the dispatcher fix."}

file.content:
# Lookup dispatch

# buz_exec_lookup() - Resolve colophon via registry and exec
# Args: colophon, base_dir [, extra args passed through to exec]
# Execs: BUZ_FOLIO=<folio> ${base_dir}/${module} ${command} [extra args]
# Dies if colophon not found
buz_exec_lookup() {
  zbuz_sentinel

  local z_colophon="${1:-}"
  local z_base_dir="${2:-}"
  test -n "${z_colophon}" || buc_die "buz_exec_lookup: colophon required"
  test -n "${z_base_dir}" || buc_die "buz_exec_lookup: base_dir required"
  shift 2

  # Find colophon in registry
  local z_found=""
  local z_i=""
  for z_i in "${!z_buz_colophon_roll[@]}"; do
    test "${z_buz_colophon_roll[z_i]}" = "${z_colophon}" || continue
    z_found="${z_i}"
    break
  done
  test -n "${z_found}" || buc_die "buz_exec_lookup: colophon not found: ${z_colophon}"

  # Decode folio from channel
  local z_folio=""
  local z_args=("$@")
  case "${z_buz_channel_roll[z_found]}" in
    "")
      if (( ${#z_args[@]} )); then
        buc_warn "Colophon '${z_colophon}' takes no folio; ignoring unexpected argument(s): ${z_args[*]}"
        z_args=()
      fi
      ;;
    "imprint")
      z_folio="${BURD_TOKEN_3}"
      ;;
    "param1")
      if (( ${#z_args[@]} )); then
        z_folio="${z_args[0]}"
        z_args=("${z_args[@]:1}")
      fi
      ;;
    *)
      buc_die "buz_exec_lookup: unknown channel: ${z_buz_channel_roll[z_found]}"
      ;;
  esac

  # Dispatch with folio in exec environment
  BUZ_FOLIO="${z_folio}" exec "${z_base_dir}/${z_buz_module_roll[z_found]}" "${z_buz_command_roll[z_found]}" ${z_args[@]+"${z_args[@]}"}
}

# eof

content.text:
Found the convention. `param1`-channel functions read folio from the `BUZ_FOLIO` env var that the dispatch exports, **not** from `$1`. The dispatch line at `buz_zipper.sh:280` is:

```
BUZ_FOLIO="${z_folio}" exec "${z_base_dir}/${z_buz_module_roll[z_found]}" "${z_buz_command_roll[z_found]}" ${z_args[@]+"${z_args[@]}"}
```

Note `z_args` is the **remaining** args after the folio is consumed (line 271 strips index 0). So when the user passes just the JSON path, no positional args reach the function.

Working examples like `rbfd_enshrine` (line 870) use `zrbfc_resolve_vessel "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"`. The `rbgp_payor_install` function was written before this convention took hold (or was missed during a refactor) — it still reads `${1:-}`.

Fix is to switch the function to the BUZ_FOLIO convention.

input.params: {"identity": "BAAAA", "files": ["Tools/rbk/rbgp_Payor.sh"], "intent": "Third pre-existing bug surfaced by Tier 2 of ₢BAAAA (operator authorized in-scope). rbgp_payor_install was reading its folio from positional ${1:-}, but the BUK workbench dispatch convention for param1-channel enrollments (see buz_zipper.sh:280, buz_exec_lookup) is to export the folio as BUZ_FOLIO and strip it from the positional args before exec'ing the command. The 'param1' channel name describes how the OPERATOR passes the folio (as the first command-line arg to the tabtarget), not how the COMMAND function receives it. Canonical consumers like rbfd_enshrine (rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh:870) use `local -r z_X=\"${BUZ_FOLIO:-}\"`; rbgp_payor_install was the lone exception still reading $1. After this pace's earlier zipper-channel fix (enrollment '' → 'param1'), the dispatch began consuming the JSON path into BUZ_FOLIO and the function — still reading $1 — saw an empty positional arg and died at 'OAuth JSON file path required as first argument'. Fix: change `local -r z_oauth_json_file=\"${1:-}\"` to `local -r z_oauth_json_file=\"${BUZ_FOLIO:-}\"`. One-line change, single file. bash -n clean. Tier 2 retry pending."}

One small UI-text drift I notice: the title is **"Make sure you trust Recipe Bottle Payor"** with an enclosed permissions-grant section, whereas the procedure prose currently yawps **"Recipe Bottle Payor wants access"**. Possible explanations:

- Google rotates the title text per session, locale, or app-verification state.
- This is a newer variant that's gradually replacing the older "wants access" wording.
- The "wants access" header may appear deeper in the page (below the visible screenshot frame).

I'll leave the title yelp alone for now — you're the live observer and you say it's in line, so I'll trust that. If you'd like me to also update that to either variant (or note both as alternatives), say the word and I'll fold it in.

Continuing with your flow: copy the authorization code from the bottom section and paste it back into the terminal prompt. **Watch for F5 no-echo** as you paste — that's the live verification of the `buh_prompt_secret` change.

#!/bin/bash
# Copyright 2025 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# Recipe Bottle Repository Configuration - Base Values

# Resource prefixing — both required, 2–11 chars, lowercase, ending in hyphen (e.g., "prod-", "bhyslop-").
# Placeholder values below carry a visible "change-me" hint via the 'x' marker.
RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX=cancbhl-
RBRR_RUNTIME_PREFIX=canrbhl-

RBRR_DNS_SERVER=8.8.8.8

# Reference directory containing specs for all vessels: relative to project root
RBRR_VESSEL_DIR=rbev-vessels

# Container workspace path — working directory for all bottle containers
RBRR_BOTTLE_WORKSPACE=/workspace

# Depot configuration — moniker pairs with CLOUD_PREFIX to derive
# project ID, GAR repository, pool stem, and bucket at kindle.
RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER=canest2bhm100002
RBRR_GCP_REGION=us-central1

# Google Cloud Build settings
RBRR_GCB_MACHINE_TYPE=e2-standard-2
RBRR_GCB_TIMEOUT=2700s
RBRR_GCB_MIN_CONCURRENT_BUILDS=3

# Secrets directory — contains all credential files (RBRA and RBRO format)
# Credential file paths are derived as kindle constants in rbdc_DerivedConstants.sh
RBRR_SECRETS_DIR=../station-files/secrets

# Public documentation URL — target for buh_tlt glossary links.
# Update per-release (swap branch name) or per-incorporation (point at your fork).
RBRR_PUBLIC_DOCS_URL="https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md"

# eof

content.text:
I have a confusion to flag before editing.

The install ceremony output mentions **two** regime files:
- "Configuration required in **rbrp.env**" — listing four `RBRP_*` fields (Payor project / OAuth client / operator email / billing).
- "set **RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER and RBRR_GCP_REGION in rbrr.env first**" — but only as a precondition for `rbgp_depot_levy` (which isn't in our Tier 2 or Tier 3 plan).

You said "RBRR edits" — but on inspection, **both regime files already have every value the ceremony output mentioned**:

| File | Field | Current value | Ceremony's expected |
|------|-------|---------------|--------------------|
| `.rbk/rbrp.env` | `RBRP_PAYOR_PROJECT_ID` | `rbwg-p-251228075220` | ✓ matches |
| `.rbk/rbrp.env` | `RBRP_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID` | `297222692580-...qh9da` | ✓ matches |
| `.rbk/rbrp.env` | `RBRP_OPERATOR_EMAIL` | `bhyslop@gmail.com` | ✓ matches |
| `.rbk/rbrp.env` | `RBRP_BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID` | `0173BC-6A77FA-3796BC` | ✓ set (not `<obtain...>`) |
| `.rbk/rbrr.env` | `RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER` | `canest2bhm100002` | ✓ set |
| `.rbk/rbrr.env` | `RBRR_GCP_REGION` | `us-central1` | ✓ set |

Nothing in either file matches the install's "needs to be set" notice — those messages are produced unconditionally as a reminder pattern, not driven by actual missing values. **No edits required.**

A few possibilities for what you meant:

1. You skimmed the install output's `Configuration required in rbrp.env` block and asked me to apply it — but the values are already there. (No-op for me.)
2. You meant edits I should make for a specific Tier 3 step I haven't surfaced yet.
3. Typo / muscle memory — you meant a different file or field.

Tell me which, or — if you'd like me to just continue as if nothing-edits-needed — confirm and I'll do the second half of Tier 2 (the F12+F3 verification via `rbw-dl.PayorListsDepots.sh`), then move to Tier 3.

message.content: Ah, indeed, this was simply regenerating secrets, no new config.  proceed.

content.text: Continuing Tier 2 — running `rbw-dl.PayorListsDepots.sh` to exercise the F12 no-cache path and F3 refresh-token URL via the OAuth refresh flow.

content.text:
Fast suite regression check: 98 passed / 0 failed / 0 skipped — identical to pre-fix baseline. The `bud_dispatch` and zipper edits introduced no regression.

### Tier 2 complete — checkpoint before Tier 3

All Tier 2 verifications green:

| F-finding | What was verified | How |
|-----------|-------------------|-----|
| F2 (TOCTOU) | RBRO file created at `600` from the first byte | `stat` post-ceremony |
| F3 (URL constants) | All four migrated URLs reach Google live | `rbw-gPI` + `rbw-dl` both succeeded |
| F5 (no-echo) | Auth-code paste does not echo to terminal | Empirical observation |
| F12 (no-cache) | `zrbgp_authenticate_capture` runs fresh per call | Successful re-authentication in `rbw-dl` |
| (bonus) bud_dispatch fix | Doesn't break other tabtargets | Fast suite green |

### Tier 3 — the destructive credential lifecycle

This exercises F13 (RBRA private-key reality) end-to-end. The plan, with **destructive** steps flagged:

1. **`rbw-aM.PayorMantlesGovernor`** ⚠️ — deletes existing Governor SAs and creates a fresh one. Rotates `RBRA_PRIVATE_KEY` for the Governor identity. The current Governor RBRA file gets replaced. Per the docket, this writes via `rbgp_Payor.sh:~1456` — one of the three F13 sites.
2. Install the new Governor RBRA per the `buc_bare` hint emitted by mantle.
3. **`rbw-arI.GovernorInvestsRetriever <identity>`** — creates a Retriever SA. Writes RBRA via `rbgg_Governor.sh:281` (F13 site).
4. **`rbw-adI.GovernorInvestsDirector <identity>`** — creates a Director SA. Writes RBRA via the same `rbgg` site.
5. `rbw-arr` + `rbw-adr` — roster both SA sets (read-only).
6. `rbw-ft.RetrieverTalliesHallmarks` — full consumer round-trip. The Retriever's RBRA gets sourced into `rbgo_OAuth.sh:zrbgo_build_jwt_capture`, where `printf '%b'` on the multi-line PEM proves F13's "real newlines, %b is defensive" reality.

A few things to confirm before I launch:

- **Identities for invest commands**: `rbw-arI` and `rbw-adI` take an identity name as `param1`. Do you have specific identity names you want used (e.g., a moniker tied to this depot or a generic `test`)? Looking at the orient earlier, the heat doesn't mention specific names. I can default to something obvious like `bhl` (matching your existing CLOUD_PREFIX) or you can supply.
- **Time budget**: ~15-25 min total. Mantle is the slowest (rotates SA + waits for IAM consistency).
- **Final OK**: confirm you actually want to mutate the GCP state. The mechanical + Tier 2 verification is already locked in via two notches; Tier 3 buys F13 end-to-end at the cost of destructive GCP changes.

Your call. Go / hold / skip Tier 3 entirely (then we'd move to the wrap).

content.text:
Mantle aborted with HTTP 403 on the depot project lookup. Diagnosed it: the rbrr.env config points at a depot project that doesn't exist.

### The mismatch

`rbgp_governor_mantle` derives the depot project ID by concatenating `${RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX}d-${RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER}`. Your current values:

- `RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX=cancbhl-`
- `RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER=canest2bhm100002`

Derived: `cancbhl-d-canest2bhm100002` — that project doesn't exist (or you lack visibility to it), hence the 403.

### What does exist in your COMPLETE depots

Comparing against the `rbw-dl` output, your actual depots split into two prefix families:

| Family | CLOUD_PREFIX | Monikers (COMPLETE) |
|--------|--------------|---------------------|
| bhl | `cancbhl-` | `canest2bhl100000`–`canest2bhl100007` |
| bhm | `cancbhm-` | `canest2bhm100000`–`canest2bhm100002` |

The moniker `canest2bhm100002` (your current `RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER`) exists with prefix `cancbhm-`, not `cancbhl-`. So this looks like a one-character prefix drift: `cancbhl-` → `cancbhm-`.

### Two ways to fix

1. **Change `RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX` to `cancbhm-`** — keeps the existing `canest2bhm100002` moniker; targets the real `cancbhm-d-canest2bhm100002` depot (COMPLETE). Minimal change. Recommended unless you specifically wanted to target a bhl-family depot.

2. **Change `RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER`** to one of the `cancbhl-` family monikers (e.g., `canest2bhl100007`) — keeps the existing prefix. Use this if you specifically want the bhl family.

Which one (or something else entirely)? Once you say, I'll apply, regenerate context if anything in tabtarget land cares, and retry mantle.

content.text:
**F13 producer-side reality empirically confirmed**: the RBRA file is **32 lines / 1909 bytes** — a single-line RBRA would be one line of content with `\n` escape sequences inside the quoted string. The multi-line shape proves `jq -r` unescaped JSON `\n` to real newlines, the producer's `printf '%s'` preserved them, and the on-disk RBRA holds multi-line PEM verbatim.

(Side observation, out of scope: the RBRA files are mode `644`, world-readable. The F2 TOCTOU fix was scoped to RBRO; RBRA writers don't have the same umask discipline. Worth a follow-up pace but not for ₢BAAAA.)

Installing the new RBRA and proceeding.

content.subject: Pre-flight baseline
content.description: git status, shellcheck baseline, fast suite baseline

content.subject: Step 1: F1+F6a deletes, F6b shellcheck directives
content.description: Delete ZRBGO_PRIVATE_KEY_FILE (rbgo:50), delete 7 ZRBGP_INFIX_* (rbgp), add SC2153 directive to rbgp + rbgg

content.subject: Step 2: F3 OAuth URL constants
content.description: Mint RBGC_OAUTH_AUTHORIZE_URL + RBGC_OAUTH_USERINFO_URL; swap four URL sites in rbgp

content.subject: Step 3: F2 TOCTOU + F5 buh_prompt_secret
content.description: Subshell + umask 077 around RBRO write; mint buh_prompt_secret in buh_handbook; swap auth-code-paste callsite

content.subject: Step 4: F8-F12 comment-only edits
content.description: Comments on rbgu_rbro_load, RBRP_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID, scrubber, rbgp scope-tag header, zrbgp_authenticate_capture

content.subject: Step 5: F13 producer-reality docs + brace freebie
content.description: CAUTION comments at three writer sites; brace fixes at rbgu:602 and rbgg:264; consumer-comment fix at rbgo:82-83

content.subject: Tier 1 verification
content.description: bash -n on all 7 files, fast suite 93/93, shellcheck delta ~9 fewer, regime validators

content.subject: Checkpoint: Tier 1 results, await Tier 2 go
content.description: Report Tier 1 results and pause for operator confirmation before interactive Payor install

content.subject: Tier 2: interactive Payor install
content.description: rbw-gPI ceremony — confirm F5 no-echo, F2 perms 600, F3 URLs round-trip; then rbw-dl

content.subject: Checkpoint: Tier 2 results, await Tier 3 go
content.description: Report Tier 2 results and pause for operator confirmation before destructive GCP lifecycle

content.subject: Tier 3: live GCP credential lifecycle
content.description: Governor mantle → invest retriever → invest director → roster both → tally hallmarks (F13 end-to-end)

content.subject: Notch: single jjx_record
content.description: Single notch on 7 files with intent line synthesizing what was applied

content.subject: bud_dispatch fix verified via Tier 2
content.description: Confirm the one-line dispatch fix doesn't break other dispatch paths — Tier 2 itself is the strongest live test (uses both flags), then run fast suite as a regression backstop

content.subject: Fast suite regression check post-dispatch-fix
content.description: Re-run fast suite to confirm the bud_dispatch.sh interactive-branch fix didn't break other dispatch code paths

content.text:
**F13 end-to-end verified empirically.** The Retriever's multi-line PEM was sourced, fed through `printf '%b'` to openssl, signed a JWT, exchanged for an OAuth token, and queried GAR — all of which would have failed at the openssl signing step if the docket's old "RBRA contains \n escapes" claim were true. The new documentation is accurate.

"No hallmarks found" is expected — the depot is fresh, no images yet.
